examples: His mother had a patient who habitually flew into rages.
The country is in the sort of unhappy and scratchy mood that voters in mid-terms habitually take out on the president's party.
Although a man of blood and violence, Richard was too impetuous to be either treacherous or habitually cruel.
The authors habitually think of immigrants filling educationally graded slots in some pre-determined economy.
And yet, there are patterns, ways in which we habitually interact with our experiences.
